mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and declining consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, which boosts corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of occurrences or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term equity price appreciation as investors anticipate higher near-term earnings.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are negative in more than 50% of acceleration episodes or the signal fails to predict returns during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly inflation increases erode real consumer purchasing power and raise the probability of central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in more than 55% of high-inflation months or the signal loses predictive power when inflation expectations are anchored below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the labor force and supports aggregate wage growth, which drives consumer spending and corporate profit expansion, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months with positive payroll growth or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.05 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms synchronized macroeconomic growth, which reduces recession risk premiums and generates positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 55% of synchronized growth quarters or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing downturn and weakening supply-side momentum, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Short-term returns remain positive in more than 50% of consecutive decline episodes or the signal does not differentiate between temporary inventory adjustments and structural downturns.
